# MODULE 1: HYSTERESIS

## Overview
Module 1 demonstrates ferroelectric hysteresis behavior using the Preisach model. Key physics: P-E curves, remanent polarization (Pr ~25 µC/cm²), coercive field (Ec ~1 MV/cm).

### Screenshot Analysis

#### Screenshot 1: fecim_module01-hysteresis_2026-01-22_18-42-14.png
**State**: Level 1/30, P: -30.00 µC/cm², Running sine wave mode

**Physics Analysis**:
- P-E hysteresis loop correctly shows characteristic "S" shape
- Axes: P (µC/cm²) on Y-axis (-36 to +36), E (MV/cm) on X-axis (-3.0 to +3.0)
- Coercive field markers (±Ec) shown as vertical cyan lines - CORRECT
- Remanent polarization (Pr) shown as horizontal cyan line - CORRECT
- Material parameters displayed: Pr=25, Ps=30 µC/cm², Ec=1.20 MV/cm - MATCHES SPEC
- Endurance: 1e+10 cycles - realistic value

**UI/UX Analysis**:
- Dark blue theme (#003264) consistent with project spec
- Tab navigation clear: Home, 1.Hysteresis, 2.Crossbar+, etc.
- Left panel: Memory cell visualization with level indicator (good)
- Right panel: Controls well-organized in sections (MODE, TIMING, STATE, MATERIAL)
- 30-level gradient bar on right clearly shows quantization levels

**Interactive Elements**:
- Mode dropdown: "Default HZO" / "Sine Wave" - functional
- E-field slider with value display (-2.265 MV/cm)
- Freq slider: 0.50 Hz
- Trail slider: 500 (trace length)
- Pause/Reset/EL15 buttons
- WRITE button (red, prominent) - shows |E| > Ec condition
- Screenshot/Record buttons top-right

**Issues Found**:
- [MEDIUM] Legend at top (Ec, Pr, Current) uses small colored boxes - could be larger
- [LOW] "30 LEVELS / 4.9 bits" label small, could be more prominent
- [LOW] Status bar "Running | t=17.39s | Switched: 0.0%" - good info density

**UI Improvement Proposals**:
1. [HIGH] Increase legend box size from ~10px to 16px for visibility
2. [MEDIUM] Add tooltips to explain Ec, Pr, Ps for educational clarity
3. [LOW] Consider adding grid lines to P-E plot for easier value reading

---

#### Screenshot 2: fecim_module01-hysteresis_2026-01-22_18-42-15.png
**State**: Level 30/30, P: 30.00 µC/cm², Fully polarized positive

**Physics Analysis**:
- Shows complete hysteresis loop traversal (yellow trace)
- Current position at top of loop (positive saturation) - CORRECT
- Level indicator shows 30/30 with bright yellow/green color - CORRECT
- Switched: 100.0% shown in status bar - accurate

**UI/UX Analysis**:
- Memory cell visualization updates correctly (shows "30" in green box)
- Level gradient bar on right correctly highlights level 30
- Loop trace clearly visible showing history of polarization changes

**Issues Found**:
- [LOW] Trace gets visually dense when overlapping - consider trail fade

---

#### Screenshot 3: fecim_module01-hysteresis_2026-01-22_18-42-17.png
**State**: Write/Read Demo mode, Level 30/30, E-field: -0.546 MV/cm

**Physics Analysis**:
- Shows READ operation (|E| < Ec, non-destructive)
- "WRITING LEVEL 3" checkbox shows multi-level programming demo
- "This is how we STORE data" - educational text excellent
- WHY THIS MATTERS section: "vs DRAM: 1000× less energy, vs Flash: 10× faster, 30 levels = 4.9 bits/cell, Non-volatile: no refresh" - MATCHES DR. TOUR SPECS

**UI/UX Analysis**:
- READ button now shown (green) instead of WRITE
- Educational panel "WHAT YOU'RE SEEING" provides context
- Memory log shows: "t=20.3s WRITE → 3"

**Issues Found**:
- [MEDIUM] Text "Electric field E > Ec" should be "E < Ec" for READ mode - POTENTIAL BUG
- [LOW] WRITING LEVEL checkbox should perhaps say "TARGET LEVEL" for clarity

---

#### Screenshot 4: fecim_module01-hysteresis_2026-01-22_18-42-18.png
**State**: Write/Read Demo, Level 1/30, E-field: -2.069 MV/cm

**Physics Analysis**:
- Multiple minor loops visible (yellow trace) - shows partial switching behavior
- Demonstrates ability to write to intermediate levels
- P: -30.00 µC/cm² at level 1/30 - CORRECT (negative saturation)

**UI/UX Analysis**:
- Minor loops clearly distinguishable from major loop
- Shows practical demonstration of multi-level storage

---

#### Screenshot 5: fecim_module01-hysteresis_2026-01-22_18-42-32.png
**State**: Write/Read Demo, HOLDING Level 30

**Physics Analysis**:
- Shows holding operation with multiple historical traces
- Memory log shows sequence of operations (READ, WRITE operations)
- Level bar correctly at 30

**Issues Found**:
- [LOW] Memory log text quite small, may be hard to read

---

#### Screenshot 6: fecim_module01-hysteresis_2026-01-22_18-42-39.png
**State**: Write/Read Demo, WRITING 14...

**Physics Analysis**:
- Intermediate level programming demonstration
- Shows writing to level 14 of 30

---

#### Screenshot 7: fecim_module01-hysteresis_2026-01-22_18-42-42.png
**State**: Write/Read Demo, WRITING 1...

**Physics Analysis**:
- Writing to minimum level (full negative polarization)
- Minor loop visible as system approaches target level

---

#### Screenshot 8: fecim_module01-hysteresis_2026-01-22_18-43-24.png
**State**: Manual mode, Level 29/30, multiple full cycles visible

**Physics Analysis**:
- Shows extensive cycling with multiple overlapping traces
- Green, yellow, blue traces show different cycles
- Demonstrates hysteresis memory effect over time

**UI/UX Analysis**:
- Mode changed to "Manual" allowing direct field control
- Dense trace history shows endurance demonstration

**Issues Found**:
- [MEDIUM] Multiple overlapping traces hard to distinguish - consider color cycling or opacity fade

---

#### Screenshot 9: fecim_module01-hysteresis_2026-01-22_18-53-37.png
**State**: Sine Wave mode, Level 6/30, E-field: 0.720 MV/cm

**Physics Analysis**:
- Clean P-E loop with single trace
- Current position on rising branch (E increasing)
- P: -19.07 µC/cm² at level 6/30 - intermediate state correct
- Switched: 48.3% matches visual position

**UI/UX Analysis**:
- "READING" indicator with animated dots shows active state
- Clear, uncluttered view good for initial understanding

---

### Module 1 Summary

**Physics Accuracy**: 9/10
- All fundamental physics correct (hysteresis, P-E relationship, coercive field, remanent polarization)
- 30-level quantization properly implemented
- Material parameters match documented specs

**UI/UX Quality**: 8/10
- Clean, professional dark theme
- Well-organized control panels
- Educational text helpful
- Minor issues with text size and legend visibility

**Critical Issues**: 1
- Text stating "E > Ec" during READ operation may be incorrect (should be E < Ec)

**Recommended Fixes**:
1. Verify READ/WRITE condition text logic
2. Increase legend and status text size
3. Add trace fade for better visibility of recent vs old data

# MODULE 2: CROSSBAR+

## Overview
Module 2 demonstrates FeCIM crossbar arrays for matrix-vector multiplication (MVM) with non-ideality analysis. Covers: Conductance visualization, IR Drop, Sneak Paths, Drift, Input/Output, Ideal vs Actual comparison, and Accuracy Analysis.

### Screenshot Analysis

#### Screenshot 1: fecim_module02-crossbar_2026-01-22_18-44-46.png
**Tab**: Conductance (Main crossbar view)

**Physics Analysis**:
- 64x64 crossbar array displayed as heatmap
- Colorbar shows conductance levels (0-30 range for FeCIM)
- Random weight initialization visible - appropriate for demonstration
- Left panel explains: "Each cell = one FeCIM / The array enables O(1) matrix-vector multiply"

**UI/UX Analysis**:
- Sub-tabs well organized: Conductance, IR Drop, Sneak Paths, Input/Output, Ideal vs Actual, Accuracy Analysis
- Right panel controls: Actions, Array Settings, Signal Quality, Display
- Array Size slider, Noise slider (2.0%), ADC Bits: 6
- Live Metrics section shows: Accuracy (Ideal: 90.0%, Actual: 89.8%), Energy comparison, Performance

**Interactive Elements**:
- Run Enhanced MVM button (cyan, prominent)
- Reset Array button
- Export Data button
- Array Size slider (64x64)
- Noise slider (2.0%)
- ADC Bits slider (6)
- Display dropdown (plasma colormap)

**Issues Found**:
- [LOW] "30 LEVELS" label in status bar small
- [MEDIUM] Color scale max/min values not labeled on colorbar

---

#### Screenshot 2: fecim_module02-crossbar_2026-01-22_18-44-48.png
**Tab**: IR Drop Analysis

**Physics Analysis**:
- Shows voltage drop across metal lines
- Visual gradient from bright (high voltage) to dark (low voltage)
- Vertical striping pattern indicates row/column-dependent IR drop - CORRECT
- Left panel explains: "Voltage drops along wires / Resistive lines cause V to decrease / Cells far from drivers see less voltage"

**UI/UX Analysis**:
- Clear visualization of IR drop effect
- Gradient colormap (plasma) appropriate for voltage visualization

**Issues Found**:
- [MEDIUM] Actual voltage values not shown - would be educational
- [LOW] Could add contour lines for quantitative analysis

---

#### Screenshot 3: fecim_module02-crossbar_2026-01-22_18-44-50.png
**Tab**: Sneak Paths Analysis

**Physics Analysis**:
- Shows parasitic current paths in crossbar
- Yellow highlighted regions indicate high sneak path susceptibility
- Blue cross pattern shows addressed cell vs neighboring cells
- Left panel: "Parasitic currents through unselected cells / Yellow = High sneak path / Blue = Low sneak path"

**UI/UX Analysis**:
- Color scheme effectively distinguishes sneak path severity
- Grid pattern shows addressed vs unaddressed cells

**Issues Found**:
- [LOW] Legend could clarify "High" vs "Low" sneak path quantitatively

---

#### Screenshot 4: fecim_module02-crossbar_2026-01-22_18-44-52.png
**Tab**: Input/Output Visualization

**Physics Analysis**:
- Top: Input Vector (V) - blue bars showing voltage inputs
- Bottom: Output Vector (I) - orange/yellow bars showing current outputs
- Equation shown: "Crossbar W × V = I" - CORRECT MVM representation
- Status: "Complete: 4096 MACs, 38.44 pJ, 1066× better than GPU"

**UI/UX Analysis**:
- Clean bar chart visualization
- Clear input/output relationship
- Energy comparison prominent (1066× better than GPU)

**Live Metrics**:
- Accuracy: Ideal 90.0%, Actual 89.8%, Δ: -0.2%
- Energy: FeCIM 38.44 pJ, GPU 40960 pJ
- Performance: 4096 MACs, 10 ns

**Issues Found**:
- [LOW] Bar charts could use axis labels for voltage/current units

---

#### Screenshot 5: fecim_module02-crossbar_2026-01-22_18-44-55.png
**Tab**: Ideal vs Actual Comparison

**Physics Analysis**:
- Side-by-side comparison of ideal (no noise) vs actual (with non-idealities)
- Both heatmaps show 64x64 weight matrices
- Visual differences show quantization and noise effects

**UI/UX Analysis**:
- Effective side-by-side comparison layout
- Legend shows: Ideal (no noise), Actual (with noise)

**Issues Found**:
- [MEDIUM] Difference map would be more informative than side-by-side

---

#### Screenshot 6: fecim_module02-crossbar_2026-01-22_18-44-57.png
**Tab**: Accuracy Analysis (Waterfall chart)

**Physics Analysis**:
- Accuracy Degradation Analysis waterfall chart
- Shows step-by-step accuracy loss: Baseline → ADC/DAC → IR Drop → Device Variation → Sneak Paths
- Target: 87% (Dr. Tour) - MATCHES SPEC
- Left panel: "Step-by-step accuracy loss: 1. Baseline (ideal), 2. + ADC/DAC quantization, 3. + IR drop, 4. + Device variation, 5. + Sneak paths"

**UI/UX Analysis**:
- Waterfall chart clearly shows cumulative accuracy degradation
- Green (high accuracy) to olive (reduced accuracy) color progression
- "Shows where accuracy is lost and why" - excellent educational clarity

**Issues Found**:
- [MEDIUM] Actual percentage values should be labeled on each bar
- [LOW] Y-axis scale not visible

---

**Observations**:
- Noise slider adjusted from 2.0% to 7.0%
- Array size changed from 64x64 to 32x32
- ADC Bits changed from 6 to 8
- N° Operations updates: 4096 MACs → 1024 MACs for smaller array

**UI Consistency**: Good - all controls respond appropriately

- IR Drop: 8.62% Negligible

**Issues Found**:

---

#### Screenshot 12: fecim_module02-crossbar_2026-01-22_18-45-35.png
**Tab**: Sneak Paths Analysis (8x8 with selection)

**Analysis**:
- Cell [1,4] selected showing sneak path analysis
- Yellow highlighted cell = target cell
- Blue cells = affected by sneak paths
- Details: Sneak ratio, Cell properties, Mitigation options

---

#### Screenshot 13: fecim_module02-crossbar_2026-01-22_18-45-42.png
**Tab**: Accuracy Analysis (8x8 array)

**Analysis**:
- ACCURACY DEGRADATION ANALYSIS panel showing:
- Baseline (Ideal): 90.0%
- After Non-Idealities: 89.9%

- Breakdown: ADC/DAC Quantization -0.0%, IR Drop -0.0%, Device Variation -0.0%, Sneak Paths -0.0%

### Module 2 Summary

**Physics Accuracy**: 9/10
- MVM operation correctly implemented (I = G × V)
- Non-idealities (IR drop, sneak paths, device variation) properly modeled
- Energy metrics match Dr. Tour's claims (1000× better than DRAM/GPU)
- 87% accuracy target properly referenced

**UI/UX Quality**: 8/10
- Well-organized tabbed interface for different analysis views
- Interactive cell selection with detailed analysis panels
- Clear metrics display with live updates
- Minor improvements needed for axis labels and legends

**Critical Issues**: 0

**Recommended Fixes**:
1. Add axis labels and units to bar charts
2. Label colorbar min/max values
3. Add difference visualization option for Ideal vs Actual
4. Label waterfall chart bars with actual percentages
5. Improve Analysis Results panel formatting (reduce text density)

# MODULE 3: MNIST

## Overview
Module 3 demonstrates MNIST digit recognition using FeCIM crossbar arrays. Shows FP (floating-point) vs CIM (compute-in-memory) dual mode comparison with 87% accuracy target.

### Screenshot Analysis

#### Screenshot 1: fecim_module03-mnist_2026-01-22_18-45-58.png
**State**: Digit "5" displayed, inference complete

**Physics Analysis**:
- Header: "MNIST FeCIM | 784→128→10 | 30 Levels | 87%"
- Network architecture: 784 inputs (28x28 pixels) → 128 hidden → 10 outputs
- 30 FeCIM levels properly indicated
- 87% accuracy matches Dr. Tour's target

**UI Layout**:
- Top-left: Input digit display (28x28 grayscale)
- Top-right: FP vs CIM Comparison bar chart
  - Shows output activations for each digit class (0-9)
  - Bars for FP (blue) and CIM (cyan/green)
- Middle: Probability Distribution showing digit 5 correctly identified
- Bottom-left: Hardware Config panel (ADC bits, Noise, etc.)
- Bottom-right: Weights visualization (784×128 weight matrix)

**Interactive Elements**:
- Clear/Random/Inference/Demo buttons at top
- Why CIM?/HW Reality/Failure/About buttons
- Hardware Config sliders: ADC (4-8 bits), Noise (0.01-0.14)
- Weight Quantization table with layer details
- Energy Efficiency panel with comparison bar

**Issues Found**:
- [LOW] Weight visualization very dense - hard to see individual weights
- [MEDIUM] "Probability Distribution (FP vs CIM)" label small

---

#### Screenshots 2-7: Inference variations on digit "5"
**Observations**:
- Multiple inference runs showing consistent recognition
- FP and CIM results closely match
- Confidence levels vary slightly between runs (expected with noise)
- Hardware config adjustments visible in some screenshots

**Physics Verification**:
- Quantization: 784×128 = 100,352 weights, 128×10 = 1,280 weights
- Energy comparison shows FeCIM vs GPU advantage
- Status bar shows: "Sample: XX | Inference: FeCIM | GPU: XX.XX pJ | Energy: XX.X× savings"

---

#### Screenshot 8: fecim_module03-mnist_2026-01-22_18-46-25.png
**State**: Network training visible

**UI Analysis**:
- "Next CNN" label visible in Hardware Config
- Training metrics: FP=94.53%, CIM=93.59%, Agreement=98.21%, Target: 87%
- Shows actual trained accuracy exceeds Dr. Tour's 87% target

---

#### Screenshot 9: fecim_module03-mnist_2026-01-22_18-46-29.png
**State**: Layer 2 weights visible

**Physics Analysis**:
- Weights panel shows Layer 2 (128×10)
- Two-color visualization: red (negative), blue (positive) weights
- Clear separation shows learned features for each output class

**UI Analysis**:
- Legend: "Layer 1 (784×128)" and "Layer 2 (128×10)" tabs
- Weight matrix properly visualized

---

#### Screenshot 10-11: Additional inference runs

**Observations**:
- Consistent digit recognition across multiple samples
- CIM accuracy closely tracks FP accuracy

---

#### Screenshot 12: fecim_module03-mnist_2026-01-22_18-46-35.png
**State**: Digit "7" recognition

**Physics Analysis**:
- Different digit (7) correctly classified
- Shows network generalization across digit classes
- FP vs CIM comparison shows agreement

---

### Module 3 Summary

**Physics Accuracy**: 9/10
- Network architecture correctly implemented (784→128→10)
- 30-level FeCIM quantization properly applied
- Energy comparisons match Dr. Tour's claims
- Accuracy exceeds 87% target

**UI/UX Quality**: 7/10
- Information-dense layout (good for experts, may overwhelm beginners)
- Clear FP vs CIM comparison visualization
- Weight visualization too dense for detailed inspection
- Good interactive controls for hardware parameters

**Critical Issues**: 0

**Recommended Fixes**:
1. [HIGH] Add zoom/detail view for weight matrices
2. [MEDIUM] Increase font size on probability distribution labels
3. [MEDIUM] Add hover tooltips explaining hardware parameters
4. [LOW] Consider collapsible sections to reduce visual density
5. [LOW] Add confusion matrix view for accuracy analysis
